rateOutlook.source: CME FedWatch (as of 2026-03-27). Iran war escalation and oil at $112 have some traders pricing in hike possibility. Core PCE 2.7% supports cuts, but energy inflation is a wildcard

events.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
events.marketReaction: Dollar strengthened, 10-year Treasury yields rose. Prices Paid surge to 78.3 heightened inflation concerns alongside oil rally. Manufacturing expansion continues despite rising cost pressures.
events.analysis: Beat expectations (51.5) with 52.7, maintaining expansion for three consecutive months. However, Prices Paid jumped from 70.5 to 78.3, confirming that March oil surge is flowing through to manufacturing costs. While growth remains positive, inflation re-acceleration concerns further diminish Fed rate cut expectations. Manufacturing portfolios should watch for margin pressure on vulnerable companies.
watchPoints: Impact of March oil price surge ($112) on manufacturing costs
Prices subindex (70.5 last month) — further rise would intensify inflation fears
Supply chain disruption risk from Iran war

events.name: Retail Sales (February data)
events.marketReaction: Consumer discretionary stocks gained. Department stores (+3%), health/personal care (+2.3%) led strength. Overall market gains limited by oil price concerns.
events.analysis: Beat expectations with +0.6% vs +0.3% forecast, confirming consumer resilience despite February employment shock (-92K). Strongest performance in seven months shows spending durability. However, March oil surge will likely constrain future consumer spending power. Maintain consumer stock allocation but approach energy-sensitive sectors cautiously.
watchPoints: February data delayed to April 1 release
Impact of rising energy costs on consumer spending
Consumer pullback following employment shock (-92K)

events.name: Non-Farm Payrolls (NFP)
events.marketReaction: S&P 500, Nasdaq declined despite strong jobs. 10-year Treasury yields rose to 4.36%. Strong employment overshadowed by Iran war and $111 oil concerns. Good Friday shortened trading.
events.analysis: Full recovery from February shock (-92K) with +178K, reaffirming labor market resilience. Unemployment rate rose slightly to 4.3% but remains healthy. Strong employment reduces Fed rate cut pressure while facing dual headwinds of Iran war and oil surge. Job market strength supports consumer-related stocks, but inflation concerns weigh on rate-sensitive sectors.
watchPoints: Rebound from February's -92K shock is critical
Healthcare strike resolution, federal government further cuts
Unemployment rate: holding at 4.1% or rising
April 3 is Good Friday — early market close

PPI +0.7% consumer pass-through

Dot plot update (if available)

RSMus 'calm before the storm' analysis — energy pass-through is key
